llcFilesWith: Illinois Secretary of State (Form LLC-1.20, Application to Adopt an Assumed Name)
soleProprietorFilesWith: County clerk in each county where the business is conducted (Assumed Business Name Act, 805 ILCS 405)
feeBasis: Illinois prorates the state assumed-name fee by the anniversary year so it always expires in the next year divisible by five: $150 (year ending 0 or 5), $120 (1 or 6), $90 (2 or 7), $60 (3 or 8), or $30 (4 or 9), plus a payment-processor fee. It renews for $150 every five years, about $30 a year. Sole proprietors and partnerships instead pay county-set fees at the county clerk.
renewalRule: The registration runs from the filing date until the first day of the company's anniversary month in the next calendar year evenly divisible by five, then renews for successive five-year terms. Only LLCs and corporations in good standing may adopt an assumed name electronically.
protectionNote: Registering an assumed name with the Secretary of State discloses the name but grants no exclusive trademark right. It does have a practical effect: a registered assumed name is one of the records in the Section 1-10 distinguishability test, so the state will not register a later entity or assumed name that is not distinguishable from it while yours stays active. County assumed-name certificates filed by sole proprietors carry no statewide protection and are not centrally indexed.
searchNote: The Secretary of State's business search returns LLC and corporate assumed names filed with the state, because they share the entity records used for the distinguishability screen. County assumed-name certificates for sole proprietors and partnerships sit in each county clerk's records and are not in the state search.
notes: Illinois splits its DBA system. LLCs and corporations adopt an assumed name centrally with the Secretary of State (Form LLC-1.20), where it joins the state entity records and the Section 1-10 distinguishability screen. Sole proprietors and general partnerships instead file an assumed-business-name certificate with the county clerk under 805 ILCS 405 and must publish notice in a local newspaper. The state assumed-name fee is prorated so a registration always expires in the next year divisible by five.
